items.text: Every investment is silently measured against the best alternative you could have made instead. Buying stock A at a likely 9% return isn't 'making 9%' — it's LOSING 4% a year if a comparable-risk alternative offered 13%. Munger runs everything against his best available idea: 'It's your alternatives that matter.' Cash, index funds, and your existing holdings are all live alternatives.

A hurdle rate turns opportunity cost into a discipline: 'I don't part with cash for less than X% expected return.' For Indian equities a sensible personal hurdle is ~12–15% — above the ~7% risk-free bond, above the ~11–12% index expectation, or it isn't worth single-stock risk. The hurdle's power is what it makes you skip: mediocre ideas that would silently displace better future ones.

The costliest opportunity-cost error is invisible: capital parked in a 'fine' 8% idea isn't available when the fat pitch arrives at panic prices. This is why Buffett holds cash that 'yields nothing' — it's an option on future bargains. Opportunity cost isn't only about comparing today's ideas; it's about the price of being fully committed when tomorrow's better idea shows up.
Stock A offers ~9% expected return. An index fund at similar risk offers ~12%. Buying A means:
options: Effectively losing ~3%/yr versus your real alternative
items.explanation: Returns are judged against alternatives foregone. An idea must beat your best available option, not just beat zero.

items.text: Main purpose of a personal hurdle rate (say 14%)?
options: It forces you to skip mediocre ideas so capital is free for exceptional ones
items.explanation: A hurdle is a rejection filter. Its value is in the ideas it screens OUT — protecting capital for asymmetric opportunities.

items.text: Why does Buffett tolerate large cash balances 'earning nothing'?
options: Cash is an option on future bargains — its value appears at panic prices
items.explanation: Cash's return isn't its yield; it's the ability to act when others can't. The 2008-style fat pitch can only be hit with dry powder.
